root_text.sn45.56:1.1: “Sūriyassa, bhikkhave, udayato etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—aruṇuggaṁ;
root_text.sn45.56:1.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ariyassa aṭṭhaṅgikassa maggassa uppādāya etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—kalyāṇamittatā.
root_text.sn45.56:1.3: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāvessati, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarissati.
root_text.sn45.56:1.4: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkaroti?
root_text.sn45.56:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sammādiṭṭhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ …pe… sammāsamādhiṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ.
root_text.sn45.56:1.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bhāveti, ariyaṁ aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ bahulīkarotī”ti.

translation_text.sn45.56:1.1: “Mendicants, the dawn is the forerunner and precursor of the sunrise.
translation_text.sn45.56:1.2: In the same way good friendship is the forerunner and precursor of the noble eightfold path for a mendicant.
translation_text.sn45.56:1.3: A mendicant with good friends can expect to develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path.
translation_text.sn45.56:1.4: And how does a mendicant with good friends develop and cultivate the noble eightfold path?
translation_text.sn45.56:1.5: It’s when a mendicant develops right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion, which culminate in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.sn45.56:1.6: That’s how a mendicant with good friends develops and cultivates the noble eightfold path.”
